claim: AI is a universal unstructured information processor; AI = context + reasoning. Context has been underinvested relative to reasoning despite being equally critical.
actionable: When optimizing AI systems, allocate engineering effort 50-50 between improving reasoning and improving context curation, not 90-10 on reasoning.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Jeff Huber: Agentic Search

claim: Context rot is real: models degrade severely as context grows, with many frontier models becoming unreliable past 40-100k tokens despite vendor claims of 1M+ token windows.
actionable: Treat context as a finite resource with diminishing returns; never assume marketed context window = usable context window.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Jeff Huber: Agentic Search

claim: Agentic search is a loop where a model has access to search tools (hybrid search, sparse/dense vectors, regex, get-document) and can decide when to stop iterating.
actionable: Build agent-driven search loops instead of one-shot RAG; let agents iteratively refine queries, try multiple search strategies, and stop when confident.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Jeff Huber: Agentic Search

claim: Most AI failures are context failures, not reasoning failures - models like Opus are smart enough, but poor context curation breaks performance.
actionable: Debug agent failures by auditing context (what documents, tools, history were included) before assuming model capability is the bottleneck.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Jeff Huber: Agentic Search

claim: Chroma Context One is a 20B-parameter model achieving state-of-the-art agentic search at 400 tokens/sec (3000 on Cerebras), 50x smaller and ~40x cheaper than Opus.
actionable: For retrieval and search tasks, consider fine-tuned small models like Context One instead of using expensive frontier models.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Jeff Huber: Agentic Search

claim: Sub-agents are highly effective for encapsulating context and protecting the reasoning model from information overload and distraction.
actionable: Use sub-agents to gather and filter context before feeding to the main reasoning model; never feed the reasoning model raw unfiltered data.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Jeff Huber: Agentic Search

claim: Future systems will achieve continuous context: a steering layer continuously guides the reasoning model both by pull (agent asks for info) and push (layer interrupts with relevant context).
actionable: Design future agent systems to have a continuous context layer that can both respond to queries and proactively inject relevant information.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Jeff Huber: Agentic Search

claim: As model speed increases (from 40 tokens/sec to 3000+), you should push compute closer to data (like OLAP push-down) to avoid network latency costs.
actionable: When using fast inference models, co-locate search/retrieval compute with your database to minimize network round-trips.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Jeff Huber: Agentic Search

claim: Continual learning at the context layer (updating knowledge docs, not model weights) is how agents will adapt over time in production.
actionable: Build systems where agents update a context library (docs, memories, tools) as they learn, rather than trying to fine-tune model weights.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Jeff Huber: Agentic Search
